spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (window., PerformanceObserver) with runtime script (~7180 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (2 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (average, aware, category, content, contenttype, count, dashboard, duration, groups, mime, monitoring, performanceresourc).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: 15 raw hex colour(s) still in the style block. The design system requires CSS variables (`--bg-ivory`, `--text-charcoal` etc.)

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5156068351541248 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Swap raw hex colours for CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Find the remaining hex literals in the inline `<style>` and replace each with the matching variable from `public/styles.css`. Re-check WCAG AA on any new pair you create.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Adds the contentType field to PerformanceResourceTiming to hold a string corresponding to the content type of the fetched resource as returned by the server. ") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/w3c/resource-timing/pull/341 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~7180 chars of script across 2 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 2 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
